texto: Sound the tocsin of national peril and hordes of well-meaning folk with nothing much to do always materialize from nowhere. They itch to meddle in great matters of which their comprehension is usually pretty dim, and have no objection to getting their names and pictures in the papers.
explicacao: Citação de Leslie McFarlane sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Sound the tocsin of national peril and hordes of well-meaning folk with nothing much to do always…” articula reflexao, human-nature, politics em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como politics.

frase_original: Sound the tocsin of national peril and hordes of well-meaning folk with nothing much to do always materialize from nowhere. They itch to meddle in great matters of which their comprehension is usually pretty dim, and have no objection to getting their names and pictures in the papers.
